export_view.value:
Our new feature, Test Mode, is specifically crafted to streamline the onboarding process for new RepairQ users. It offers a safe environment for users to experiment with creating test data until they feel comfortable navigating the system. Once you’re ready, you can easily delete all test data, starting with tickets, followed by inventory items, and other data, ensuring a clean slate for actual business operations.

New customers will notice that Test Mode is already active when logging into their new environment. This allows you to create catalog items, inventory, tickets, Purchase orders, etc. to familiarize yourself with RepairQ. Turning off Test Mode signifies the transition to regular business, preventing further deletion of data to maintain data integrity and accurate reporting. However, users should exercise caution, as Test Mode doesn’t deactivate automatically and data can be deleted anytime, posing a potential liability if misused. After deactivating Test Mode, it can’t be turned back on to ensure your data is safe from deletion.

Note: All related data is deleted when performing a Batch Deletion. This is not limited to the data created while in Test Mode. We recommend deleting data at your discretion before turning off Test Mode and starting normal business operations. Admins are responsible for any data loss incurred, underscoring the importance of mindful record-keeping practices.

Additional Resources

  • RepairQ Subscription Plans 2024

  • RepairQ Onboarding

  • RepairQ Account Setup

Access Test Mode Settings

Only users with Admin responsibilities can access the following settings.

Note: After deactivating Test Mode, it can’t be turned back on to ensure your data is safe from deletion. If you are deleting test data, do so before deactivating Test Mode.

When Test Mode is active, you will see a banner along the page, under the Navigation Bar. While active, you can delete any Tickets, Catalog Items, Inventory levels, etc. created to familiarize yourself with RepairQ. Click the Test Mode link to view the settings page.

Alternatively, go to General Settings under the Account Section of the Company Settings page.

Disable Test Mode

Use the toggle to turn off Test Mode then Save your settings.

Deleting Test Data

We recommend deleting data at your discretion, and Admins are responsible for any data loss incurred, underscoring the importance of mindful record-keeping practices.
Note: All data is deleted when performing a Batch Deletion. This is not limited to the data created while in Test Mode.

When test mode is active and you are viewing the Account General Settings page, there will be a section for Batch Deletion. Deleting all information is a multi-step process only performed by deleting the previous data. For instance, inventory items can only be deleted after deleting tickets, purchase orders can only be deleted after deleting all inventory items, etc.

Each time you attempt to erase a batch of data, you will receive a confirmation pop-up acknowledging that you are sure you want to delete the data. Click OK to delete. After you have decided which data to delete, you can turn off Test Mode and resume regular business.

START 1.20.X
